for default fields \"error\" and \"message\"\n197\t//   - If provided, checks for the specified primary and secondary field names\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns true if the response contains at least one of the expected error fields with a non-empty value,\n200\t// false otherwise.\n201\t//\n202\t// Example usage:\n203\t//\n204\t//\t// Using default field names (checks for \"error\" and \"message\")\n205\t//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"Invalid input\"}\n206\t//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, nil)\n207\t//\n208\t//\t// Using custom field names\n209\t//\tcustomFields := &ErrorResponseFieldNames{PrimaryFieldName: \"detail\", SecondaryFieldName: \"description\"}\n210\t//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"detail\": \"Resource not found\"}\n211\t//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, customFields)\n212\tfunc ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(response interface{}, fieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames) bool {\n213\t\tif response == nil {\n214\t\t\treturn false\n215\t\t}\n216\t\n217\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n218\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n219\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\t// Try to convert to map for field checking\n223\t\trespMap, ok := response.(map[string]interface{})\n224\t\tif !ok {\n225\t\t\treturn false\n226\t\t}\n227\t\n228\t\t// Check primary field\n229\t\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n230\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n231\t\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n232\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n233\t\t\t\t}\n234\t\t\t}\n235\t\t}\n236\t\n237\t\t// Check secondary field\n238\t\tif fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n239\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n240\t\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n241\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n242\t\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\t}\n244\t\t}\n245\t\n246\t\treturn false\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\t// isNonEmptyValue checks if a value is non-empty and meaningful.\n250\t// Returns true for strings with content, numbers (non-zero), and true booleans.\n251\t// Returns false for empty strings, zero values, false booleans, and nil.\n252\tfunc isNonEmptyValue(value interface{}) bool {\n253\t\tif value == nil {\n254\t\t\treturn false\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n258\t\tcase string:\n259\t\t\treturn v != \"\"\n260\t\tcase int:\n261\t\t\treturn v != 0\n262\t\tcase int8:\n263\t\t\treturn v != 0\n264\t\tcase int16:\n265\t\t\treturn v != 0\n266\t\tcase int32:\n267\t\t\treturn v != 0\n268\t\tcase int64:\n269\t\t\treturn v != 0\n270\t\tcase uint:\n271\t\t\treturn v != 0\n272\t\tcase uint8:\n273\t\t\treturn v != 0\n274\t\tcase uint16:\n275\t\t\treturn v != 0\n276\t\tcase uint32:\n277\t\t\treturn v != 0\n278\t\tcase uint64:\n279\t\t\treturn v != 0\n280\t\tcase float32:\n281\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n282\t\tcase float64:\n283\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n284\t\tcase bool:\n285\t\t\treturn v\n286\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n287\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n288\t\tcase []interface{}:\n289\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n290\t\tdefault:\n291\t\t\treturn true\n292\t\t}\n293\t}\n294\t\n295\t// CORSConfig specifies expected CORS header values for validation.\n296\ttype CORSConfig struct {\n297\t\t// AllowOrigin is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.\n298\t\t// Use \"*\" for wildcard CORS, or specify an exact origin like \"https://example.com\".\n299\t\t// If empty, the origin header is not validated.\n300\t\tAllowOrigin string\n301\t\n302\t\t// AllowMethods is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.\n303\t\t// If empty, the methods header is not validated.\n304\t\tAllowMethods string\n305\t\n306\t\t// AllowHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.\n307\t\t// If empty, the headers header is not validated.\n308\t\tAllowHeaders string\n309\t\n310\t\t// AllowCredentials is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.\n311\t\t// If true, expects \"true\"; if false, expects \"true\" or header not present.\n312\t\t// This is typically used with specific origins (not wildcard).\n313\t\tAllowCredentials bool\n314\t\n315\t\t// ExposeHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Expose-Headers.\n316\t\t// If empty, the expose headers header is not validated.\n317\t\tExposeHeaders string\n318\t\n319\t\t// MaxAge is the expected value for Access-Control-Max-Age.\n320\t\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\n321\t\tMaxAge string\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\n325\t// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\n326\t//\n327\t// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\n328\t// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\n329\t//\n330\t// Parameters:\n331\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n332\t//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\n333\t//\n334\t// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\n335\t// Returns false if the response is nil.\n336\t//\n337\t// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\n338\t// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\n339\t//\n340\t// Example usage:\n341\t//\n342\t//\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\n343\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\n344\t//\t    // CORS headers are present\n345\t//\t}\n346\t//\n347\t//\t// Validate specific origin\n348\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\"}\n349\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n350\t//\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\n351\t//\t}\n352\t//\n353\t//\t// Validate wildcard CORS\n354\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"*\", AllowCredentials: false}\n355\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n356\t//\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\n357\t//\t}\n358\t//\n359\t//\t// Validate full CORS configuration\n360\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n361\t//\t    AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\",\n362\t//\t    AllowMethods: \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n363\t//\t    AllowHeaders: \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n364\t//\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n365\t//\t}\n366\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n367\t//\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\n368\t//\t}\n369\tfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\n370\t\tif resp == nil {\n371\t\t\treturn false\n372\t\t}\n373\t\n374\t\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\n375\t\tif config == nil {\n376\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n377\t\t\treturn origin != \"\"\n378\t\t}\n379\t\n380\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\n381\t\tif config.AllowOrigin != \"\" {\n382\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n383\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n384\t\t\t\treturn false\n385\t\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\n388\t\t\tif config.AllowOrigin == \"*\" {\n389\t\t\t\t// Wildcard is valid\n390\t\t\t\tif origin != \"*\" {\n391\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n392\t\t\t\t}\n393\t\t\t} else {\n394\t\t\t\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\n395\t\t\t\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\n396\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n397\t\t\t\t}\n398\t\t\t}\n399\t\t}\n400\t\n401\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\n402\t\tif config.AllowMethods != \"\" {\n403\t\t\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n404\t\t\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\n405\t\t\t\treturn false\n406\t\t\t}\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\n410\t\tif config.AllowHeaders != \"\" {\n411\t\t\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n412\t\t\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\n413\t\t\t\treturn false\n414\t\t\t}\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\n418\t\tif config.AllowCredentials {\n419\t\t\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\")\n420\t\t\tif credentials != \"true\" {\n421\t\t\t\treturn false\n422\t\t\t}\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\n426\t\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \"\" {\n427\t\t\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\")\n428\t\t\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\n429\t\t\t\treturn false\n430\t\t\t}\n431\t\t}\n432\t\n433\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\n434\t\tif config.MaxAge != \"\" {\n435\t\t\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\")\n436\t\t\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\n437\t\t\t\treturn false\n438\t\t\t}\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn true\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// =============================================================================\n445\t// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\n446\t// =============================================================================\n447\t\n448\t// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\n449\ttype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n450\t\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\n451\t\tPattern string\n452\t\n453\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\n454\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n455\t\n456\t\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\n457\t\tMatchAny bool\n458\t\n459\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n460\t\t// If empty, checks default fields: \"error\", \"message\"\n461\t\tFieldNames []string\n462\t}\n463\t\n464\t// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\n465\t//\n466\t// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\n467\t// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\n468\t// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\n469\t//\n470\t// Parameters:\n471\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n472\t//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\n473\t//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\n474\t//\n475\t// Returns:\n476\t//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\n477\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\n478\t//\n479\t// Example usage:\n480\t//\n481\t//\t// Check for \"not found\" error (case-insensitive)\n482\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\n483\t//\n484\t//\t// Check for \"invalid.*token\" pattern\n485\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\n486\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\n487\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n488\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n489\t\t}\n490\t\n491\t\tif pattern == \"\" {\n492\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n493\t\t}\n494\t\n495\t\t// Parse JSON body\n496\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n497\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n498\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n499\t\t}\n500\t\n501\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n502\t\tregexPattern := pattern\n503\t\tif caseInsensitive {\n504\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + pattern\n505\t\t}\n506\t\n507\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n508\t\tif err != nil {\n509\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n510\t\t}\n511\t\n512\t\t// Check default error fields\n513\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n514\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n515\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n516\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n517\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n518\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n519\t\t\t\t\t}\n520\t\t\t\t}\n521\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n522\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n523\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n524\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\n525\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n526\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n527\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n528\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t\t\t}\n530\t\t\t\t}\n531\t\t\t}\n532\t\t}\n533\t\n534\t\treturn false, nil\n535\t}\n536\t\n537\t// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\n538\t//\n539\t// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\n540\t// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\n541\t// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\n542\t// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\n543\t//\n544\t// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\n545\t// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\), it's treated as regex\n546\t// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\n547\t//\n548\t// Parameters:\n549\t//   - response: Raw response body bytes\n550\t//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\n551\t//\n552\t// Returns:\n553\t//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\n554\t//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\n555\t//\n556\t// Example usage:\n557\t//\n558\t//\t// Regex pattern matching\n559\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*token\")\n560\t//\tif err != nil {\n561\t//\t    // Pattern not found\n562\t//\t}\n563\t//\n564\t//\t// Simple substring matching\n565\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"not found\")\n566\t//\tif err != nil {\n567\t//\t    // Substring not found\n568\t//\t}\n569\t//\n570\t//\t// Common error patterns\n571\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"unauthorized\")        // OAuth errors\n572\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*credentials\") // Auth errors\n573\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"rate.*limit\")          // Rate limiting\n574\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"timeout\")               // Timeout errors\n575\t//\n576\t// # Common Error Pattern Examples\n577\t//\n578\t// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\n579\t//\n580\t// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\n581\t//\n582\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"error_description\": \"The access token expired\"}`)\n583\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid_token\")  // substring match\n584\t//\n585\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`)\n586\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"access_denied|invalid_grant\")  // regex match\n587\t//\n588\t// # Authentication Errors\n589\t//\n590\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\"}`)\n591\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"authentication.*failed\")  // regex match\n592\t//\n593\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Invalid username or password\"}`)\n594\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*credentials\")  // regex match\n595\t//\n596\t// # Authorization Errors\n597\t//\n598\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Permission denied\"}`)\n599\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"permission.*denied\")  // regex match\n600\t//\n601\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"User is not authorized to access this resource\"}`)\n602\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not.*authorized\")  // regex match\n603\t//\n604\t// # Validation Errors\n605\t//\n606\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Validation failed: required field missing\"}`)\n607\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"validation.*failed\")  // regex match\n608\t//\n609\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\"}`)\n610\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*input\")  // regex match\n611\t//\n612\t// # Resource Errors\n613\t//\n614\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Resource not found\"}`)\n615\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not found\")  // substring match\n616\t//\n617\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"User does not exist\"}`)\n618\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"does not exist\")  // substring match\n619\t//\n620\t// # Rate Limiting Errors\n621\t//\n622\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}`)\n623\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"rate.*limit\")  // regex match\n624\t//\n625\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Too many requests, please try again later\"}`)\n626\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"too many requests\")  // substring match\n627\t//\n628\t// # Timeout Errors\n629\t//\n630\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Request timeout\"}`)\n631\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n632\t//\n633\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Connection timeout after 30 seconds\"}`)\n634\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n635\t//\n636\t// # Server Errors\n637\t//\n638\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Internal server error\"}`)\n639\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"internal.*error\")  // regex match\n640\t//\n641\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Service temporarily unavailable\"}`)\n642\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"unavailable\")  // substring match\n643\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(response []byte, expectedPattern string) error {\n644\t\t// Validate inputs\n645\t\tif len(response) == 0 {\n646\t\t\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n647\t\t\t\t\"error_message\",\n648\t\t\t\t\"non-empty response\",\n649\t\t\t\t\"\",\n650\t\t\t\t\"\",\n651\t\t\t\t\"\",\n652\t\t\t\t\"\",\n653\t\t\t\t\"\",\n654\t\t\t\tnil,\n655\t\t\t\t\"\",\n656\t\t\t\t\"\",\n657\t\t\t\t[]string{\"Response body is empty\", \"Cannot validate error message pattern against empty response\"},\n658\t\t\t)\n659\t\t\treturn ve\n660\t\t}\n661\t\n662\t\tif expectedPattern == \"\" {\n663\t\t\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n664\t\t\t\t\"error_message\",\n665\t\t\t\t\"non-empty pattern\",\n666\t\t\t\t\"\",\n667\t\t\t\t\"\",\n668\t\t\t\t\"\",\n669\t\t\t\t\"\",\n670\t\t\t\t\"\",\n671\t\t\t\tnil,\n672\t\t\t\t\"\",\n673\t\t\t\t\"\",\n674\t\t\t\t[]string{\"Expected pattern cannot be empty\", \"Provide a pattern to match against error messages\"},\n675\t\t\t)\n676\t\t\treturn ve\n677\t\t}\n678\t\n679\t\t// Parse JSON body\n680\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n681\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(response, &body); err != nil {\n682\t\t\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n683\t\t\t\t\"error_message\",\n684\t\t\t\t\"valid JSON\",\n685\t\t\t\t\"\",\n686\t\t\t\t\"\",\n687\t\t\t\t\"\",\n688\t\t\t\t\"\",\n689\t\t\t\t\"\",\n690\t\t\t\tnil,\n691\t\t\t\t\"\",\n692\t\t\t\t\"\",\n693\t\t\t\t[]string{\"Failed to parse response body\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Parse error: %v\", err), \"Ensure response contains valid JSON\"},\n694\t\t\t)\n695\t\t\treturn ve\n696\t\t}\n697\t\n698\t\t// Search for error messages in common fields\n699\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n700\t\tvar foundMessages []string\n701\t\n702\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n703\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n704\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n705\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\n706\t\t\t\t}\n707\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n708\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n709\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n710\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n711\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\n712\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n713\t\t\t\t\t}\n714\t\t\t\t}\n715\t\t\t}\n716\t\t}\n717\t\n718\t\t// If no error messages found, return detailed error with response snippet\n719\t\tif len(foundMessages) == 0 {\n720\t\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n721\t\t\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n722\t\t\t\t\"error_message\",\n723\t\t\t\t\"error message field\",\n724\t\t\t\t\"\",\n725\t\t\t\t\"\",\n726\t\t\t\tsnippet,\n727\t\t\t\t\"\",\n728\t\t\t\t\"\",\n729\t\t\t\tnil,\n730\t\t\t\t\"\",\n731\t\t\t\t\"\",\n732\t\t\t\t[]string{\n733\t\t\t\t\t\"No error message found in response body\",\n734\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Checked fields: %s\", strings.Join(defaultFields, \", \")),\n735\t\t\t\t\t\"Response may not contain standard error fields\",\n736\t\t\t\t},\n737\t\t\t)\n738\t\t\treturn ve\n739\t\t}\n740\t\n741\t\t// Detect if pattern is regex or substring\n742\t\tisRegex := containsRegexMetacharacters(expectedPattern)\n743\t\n744\t\t// Check each found message for pattern match\n745\t\tfor _, message := range foundMessages {\n746\t\t\tvar matched bool\n747\t\n748\t\t\tif isRegex {\n749\t\t\t\t// Try regex matching\n750\t\t\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(expectedPattern)\n751\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n752\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern '%s': %w\", expectedPattern, err)\n753\t\t\t\t}\n754\t\t\t\tmatched = re.MatchString(message)\n755\t\t\t} else {\n756\t\t\t\t// Simple substring matching (case-sensitive)\n757\t\t\t\tmatched = strings.Contains(message, expectedPattern)\n758\t\t\t}\n759\t\n760\t\t\tif matched {\n761\t\t\t\t// Pattern found - return nil (success)\n762\t\t\t\treturn nil\n763\t\t\t}\n764\t\t}\n765\t\n766\t\t// Pattern not found in any messages - return descriptive error\n767\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n768\t\tfirstMessage := foundMessages[0]\n769\t\tif len(foundMessages) > 1 {\n770\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"pattern '%s' not found in error messages. First message: \\\"%s\\\". Response snippet: %s\",\n771\t\t\t\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\n772\t\t}\n773\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"pattern '%s' not found in error message: \\\"%s\\\". Response snippet: %s\",\n774\t\t\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\n775\t}\n776\t\n777\t// containsRegexMetacharacters checks if a string contains regex metacharacters.\n778\t// This is used to auto-detect whether a pattern should be treated as regex or substring.\n779\tfunc containsRegexMetacharacters(pattern string) bool {\n780\t\tregexChars := []string{\".\", \"*\", \"+\", \"?\", \"^\", \"$\", \"{\", \"}\", \"[\", \"]\", \"(\", \")\", \"|\", \"\\\\\"}\n781\t\tfor _, char := range regexChars {\n782\t\t\tif strings.Contains(pattern, char) {\n783\t\t\t\treturn true\n784\t\t\t}\n785\t\t}\n786\t\treturn false\n787\t}\n788\t\n789\t// extractResponseSnippet creates a truncated snippet of the response body for error messages.\n790\t// It limits the snippet to 200 characters to keep error messages readable.\n791\tfunc extractResponseSnippet(response []byte) string {\n792\t\t// Convert to string and truncate\n793\t\tresponseStr := string(response)\n794\t\tmaxLen := 200\n795\t\tif len(responseStr) > maxLen {\n796\t\t\tresponseStr = responseStr[:maxLen] + \"...\"\n797\t\t}\n798\t\t// Replace newlines with spaces for single-line error message\n799\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\n\", \" \")\n800\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\r\", \" \")\n801\t\treturn responseStr\n802\t}\n803\t\n804\t// ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig validates error messages using advanced pattern configuration.\n805\t//\n806\t// This function provides flexible error message validation with support for:\n807\t// - Custom field selection\n808\t// - Case-insensitive matching\n809\t// - Match-any vs match-all semantics\n810\t// - Multiple pattern checking\n811\t//\n812\t// Parameters:\n813\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n814\t//   - config: Pattern matching configuration\n815\t//\n816\t// Returns:\n817\t//   - true if validation succeeds, false otherwise\n818\t//   - error if configuration is invalid or JSON parsing fails\n819\t//\n820\t// Example usage:\n821\t//\n822\t//\tconfig := &ErrorMessagePattern{\n823\t//\t    Pattern:         \"unauthorized\",\n824\t//\t    CaseInsensitive: true,\n825\t//\t    FieldNames:      []string{\"error\", \"detail\"},\n826\t//\t}\n827\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(body, config)\n828\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes []byte, config *ErrorMessagePattern) (bool, error) {\n829\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n830\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n831\t\t}\n832\t\n833\t\tif config == nil {\n834\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"config cannot be nil\")\n835\t\t}\n836\t\n837\t\tif config.Pattern == \"\" {\n838\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n839\t\t}\n840\t\n841\t\t// Parse JSON body\n842\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n843\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n844\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n845\t\t}\n846\t\n847\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n848\t\tregexPattern := config.Pattern\n849\t\tif config.CaseInsensitive {\n850\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + config.Pattern\n851\t\t}\n852\t\n853\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n854\t\tif err != nil {\n855\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n856\t\t}\n857\t\n858\t\t// Determine which fields to check\n859\t\tfieldsToCheck := config.FieldNames\n860\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n861\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n862\t\t}\n863\t\n864\t\t// Check each specified field\n865\t\tmatchedFields := []string{}\n866\t\tfor _, field := range fieldsToCheck {\n867\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n868\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n869\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n870\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n871\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n872\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n873\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n874\t\t\t\t\t}\n875\t\t\t\t}\n876\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n877\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n878\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n879\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n880\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n881\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n882\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n883\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n884\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n885\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n886\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n887\t\t\t\t\t}\n888\t\t\t\t}\n889\t\t\t}\n890\t\t}\n891\t\n892\t\t// For match-all semantics, require all specified fields to match\n893\t\tif !config.MatchAny {\n894\t\t\treturn len(matchedFields) == len(fieldsToCheck), nil\n895\t\t}\n896\t\n897\t\treturn false, nil\n898\t}\n899\t\n900\t// =============================================================================\n901\t// SPECIFIC ERROR CODE VALIDATION\n902\t// =============================================================================\n903\t\n904\t// ErrorCodeInResponse checks if a response body contains a specific error code.\n905\t//\n906\t// This function searches for error codes in common error response fields.\n907\t// It supports both simple string error codes and nested error objects.\n908\t//\n909\t// Parameters:\n910\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n911\t//   - expectedCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"INVALID_TOKEN\", \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n912\t//\n913\t// Returns:\n914\t//   - true if the error code is found in the response, false otherwise\n915\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n916\t//\n917\t// Example usage:\n918\t//\n919\t//\t// Check for \"AUTH_FAILED\" error code\n920\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n921\t//\n922\t//\t// Check for OAuth \"invalid_token\" error\n923\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\")\n924\tfunc ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes []byte, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n925\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n926\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n927\t\t}\n928\t\n929\t\tif expectedCode == \"\" {\n930\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"expected code cannot be empty\")\n931\t\t}\n932\t\n933\t\t// Parse JSON body\n934\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n935\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n936\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n937\t\t}\n938\t\n939\t\t// Common error code field names\n940\t\terrorCodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n941\t\n942\t\t// Check for error code in standard fields\n943\t\tfor _, field := range errorCodeFields {\n944\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n945\t\t\t\t// Check for string match\n946\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n947\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n948\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n949\t\t\t\t\t}\n950\t\t\t\t}\n951\t\t\t\t// Check for nested error code in error object\n952\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n953\t\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n954\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok {\n955\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n956\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n957\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n958\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n959\t\t\t\t\t}\n960\t\t\t\t}\n961\t\t\t}\n962\t\t}\n963\t\n964\t\t// Check for OAuth-style error field (contains error code directly)\n965\t\tif errorCode, exists := body[\"error\"]; exists {\n966\t\t\tif strValue, ok := errorCode.(string); ok {\n967\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n968\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n969\t\t\t\t}\n970\t\t\t}\n971\t\t}\n972\t\n973\t\treturn false, nil\n974\t}\n975\t\n976\t// ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode validates both status code and error code in a single check.\n977\t//\n978\t// This is a convenience function that validates HTTP status code and error code\n979\t// together, ensuring that both the HTTP layer and application layer error\n980\t// information are consistent.\n981\t//\n982\t// Parameters:\n983\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n984\t//   - expectedStatus: Expected HTTP status code\n985\t//   - expectedCode: Expected error code in response body\n986\t//\n987\t// Returns:\n988\t//   - true if both status code and error code match, false otherwise\n989\t//   - error if body parsing fails\n990\t//\n991\t// Example usage:\n992\t//\n993\t//\t// Validate 401 response with \"UNAUTHORIZED\" error code\n994\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 401, \"UNAUTHORIZED\")\n995\t//\n996\t//\t// Validate 403 response with \"FORBIDDEN\" error code\n997\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 403, \"FORBIDDEN\")\n998\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp *http.Response, expectedStatus int, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n999\t\tif resp == nil {\n1000\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response is nil\")\n1001\t\t}\n1002\t\n1003\t\t// Check status code\n1004\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n1005\t\t\treturn false, nil\n1006\t\t}\n1007\t\n1008\t\t// Read response body\n1009\t\tvar bodyBytes []byte\n1010\t\tif resp.Body != nil {\n1011\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n1012\t\t\tvar err error\n1013\t\t\tbodyBytes, err = io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n1014\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1015\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n1016\t\t\t}\n1017\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\t// Check error code\n1020\t\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes, expectedCode)\n1021\t\tif err != nil {\n1022\t\t\treturn false, err\n1023\t\t}\n1024\t\n1025\t\treturn found, nil\n1026\t}\n1027\t\n1028\t// GetErrorMessage extracts the error message from a response body.\n1029\t//\n1030\t// This helper function searches for error messages in common fields and returns\n1031\t// the first non-empty message found.\n1032\t//\n1033\t// Parameters:\n1034\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n1035\t//\n1036\t// Returns:\n1037\t//   - The extracted error message, or empty string if not found\n1038\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n1039\t//\n1040\t// Example usage:\n1041\t//\n1042\t//\tmessage, err := GetErrorMessage(body)\n1043\t//\tif message != \"\" {\n1044\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error: %s\\n\", message)\n1045\t//\t}\n1046\tfunc GetErrorMessage(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n1047\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1048\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1049\t\t}\n1050\t\n1051\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1052\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1053\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1054\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1055\t\t}\n1056\t\n1057\t\t// Check common message fields\n1058\t\tmessageFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n1059\t\tfor _, field := range messageFields {\n1060\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1061\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1062\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1063\t\t\t\t}\n1064\t\t\t\t// Check nested message in error object\n1065\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1066\t\t\t\t\tif messageValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n1067\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := messageValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1068\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1069\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1070\t\t\t\t\t}\n1071\t\t\t\t}\n1072\t\t\t}\n1073\t\t}\n1074\t\n1075\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1076\t}\n1077\t\n1078\t// GetErrorCode extracts the error code from a response body.\n1079\t//\n1080\t// This helper function searches for error codes in common fields and returns\n1081\t// the first non-empty code found.\n1082\t//\n1083\t// Parameters:\n1084\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n1085\t//\n1086\t// Returns:\n1087\t//   - The extracted error code, or empty string if not found\n1088\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n1089\t//\n1090\t// Example (string, error) {\n1097\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1098\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1099\t\t}\n1100\t\n1101\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1102\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1103\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1104\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1105\t\t}\n1106\t\n1107\t\t// Check common code fields\n1108\t\tcodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\"}\n1109\t\tfor _, field := range codeFields {\n1110\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1111\t\t\t\t// Direct string code\n1112\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1113\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1114\t\t\t\t}\n1115\t\t\t\t// Check nested code in error object\n1116\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1117\t\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n1118\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1119\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1120\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1121\t\t\t\t\t}\n1122\t\t\t\t}\n1123\t\t\t}\n1124\t\t}\n1125\t\n1126\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1127\t}\n1128\t\n1129\t// =============================================================================\n1130\t// DETAILED VALIDATION RESULTS\n1131\t// =============================================================================\n1132\t\n1133\t// StatusCodeValidationResult provides detailed information about status code validation.\n1134\t// It includes not just whether validation passed, but also the expected vs actual values\n1135\t// and detailed mismatch information to help diagnose validation failures.\n1136\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n1137\t\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n1138\t\tValid bool\n1139\t\t// ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\n1140\t\tActualCode int\n1141\t\t// ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s) that were validated against\n1142\t\tExpectedCodes []int\n1143\t\t// MatchedCode is the specific expected code that matched (if any)\n1144\t\tMatchedCode *int\n1145\t\t// MismatchDetails contains human-readable information about why validation failed\n1146\t\tMismatchDetails string\n1147\t\t// IsClientError indicates whether the actual code is a client error (4xx)\n1148\t\tIsClientError bool\n1149\t\t// IsServerError indicates whether the actual code is a server error (5xx)\n1150\t\tIsServerError bool\n1151\t\t// Category describes the general category of the actual status code\n1152\t\tCategory string\n1153\t}\n1154\t\n1155\t// ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails performs comprehensive status code validation with detailed results.\n1156\t// Unlike HTTPStatusCodeIsValid which returns only a boolean, this function provides detailed information\n1157\t// about the validation process, making it easier to diagnose why validation failed.\n1158\t//\n1159\t// Parameters:\n1160\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1161\t//   - expected: Expected status code(s) - can be a single int or []int\n1162\t//   - requestContext: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"POST /api/users\", \"GET /api/resource/123\")\n1163\t//\n1164\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1165\t//\n1166\t// Example usage:\n1167\t//\n1168\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, []int{200, 201, 204}, \"POST /api/users\")\n1169\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1170\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %s\", result.MismatchDetails)\n1171\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Got: %d, expected one of: %v\", result.ActualCode, result.ExpectedCodes)\n1172\t//\t}\n1173\t//\n1174\t//\t// Single code validation without context\n1175\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, 200, \"\")\n1176\t//\tif result.Valid {\n1177\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code is valid: %d\", result.ActualCode)\n1178\t//\t}\n1179\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}, requestContext ...string) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1180\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1181\t\t\tValid:             false,\n1182\t\t\tActualCode:        0,\n1183\t\t\tExpectedCodes:     []int{},\n1184\t\t\tMatchedCode:       nil,\n1185\t\t\tMismatchDetails:   \"\",\n1186\t\t\tIsClientError:     false,\n1187\t\t\tIsServerError:     false,\n1188\t\t\tCategory:          \"unknown\",\n1189\t\t}\n1190\t\n1191\t\tif resp == nil {\n1192\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1193\t\t\treturn result\n1194\t\t}\n1195\t\n1196\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1197\t\n1198\t\t// Set error flags and category\n1199\t\tswitch {\n1200\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500:\n1201\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1202\t\t\tresult.Category = \"client_error\"\n1203\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600:\n1204\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1205\t\t\tresult.Category = \"server_error\"\n1206\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 300:\n1207\t\t\tresult.Category = \"success\"\n1208\t\tcase resp.StatusCode >= 300 && resp.StatusCode < 400:\n1209\t\t\tresult.Category = \"redirection\"\n1210\t\tdefault:\n1211\t\t\tresult.Category = \"other\"\n1212\t\t}\n1213\t\n1214\t\t// Parse expected codes\n1215\t\tvar expectedCodes []int\n1216\t\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n1217\t\tcase int:\n1218\t\t\texpectedCodes = []int{v}\n1219\t\tcase []int:\n1220\t\t\texpectedCodes = v\n1221\t\tdefault:\n1222\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid expected type: %T\", expected)\n1223\t\t\treturn result\n1224\t\t}\n1225\t\n1226\t\tresult.ExpectedCodes = expectedCodes\n1227\t\n1228\t\t// Check for match\n1229\t\tfor _, code := range expectedCodes {\n1230\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == code {\n1231\t\t\t\tresult.Valid = true\n1232\t\t\t\tresult.MatchedCode = &code\n1233\t\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"\"\n1234\t\t\t\treturn result\n1235\t\t\t}\n1236\t\t}\n1237\t\n1238\t\t// Extract optional context\n1239\t\tcontext := \"\"\n1240\t\tif len(requestContext) > 0 && requestContext[0] != \"\" {\n1241\t\t\tcontext = requestContext[0]\n1242\t\t}\n1243\t\n1244\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch information with suggestions\n1245\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(\n1246\t\t\texpectedCodes, resp.StatusCode, result.Category, result.IsClientError, result.IsServerError, context)\n1247\t\n1248\t\treturn result\n1249\t}\n1250\t\n1251\t// buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails creates detailed mismatch information including\n1252\t// status code category, context, and specific suggestions based on the actual code.\n1253\t// It uses FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting across the codebase.\n1254\tfunc buildComprehensiveMismatchDetails(expectedCodes []int, actualCode int, category string, isClientError, isServerError bool, context string) string {\n1255\t\t// Build validation details that include status code category information\n1256\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n1257\t\n1258\t\t// Add status code category information to validation details\n1259\t\tcategoryDesc := getCategoryDescription(category)\n1260\t\tif categoryDesc != \"\" {\n1261\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code category: %s\", categoryDesc))\n1262\t\t}\n1263\t\n1264\t\t// Prepare range info with category details\n1265\t\tvar rangeInfo string\n1266\t\tif isClientError {\n1267\t\t\trangeInfo = \"400-499 (Client Error)\"\n1268\t\t} else if isServerError {\n1269\t\t\trangeInfo = \"500-599 (Server Error)\"\n1270\t\t} else if category == \"success\" {\n1271\t\t\trangeInfo = \"200-299 (Success)\"\n1272\t\t} else if category == \"redirection\" {\n1273\t\t\trangeInfo = \"300-399 (Redirection)\"\n1274\t\t}\n1275\t\n1276\t\t// Determine expected value for FormatValidationErrorWithDetails\n1277\t\tvar expected interface{}\n1278\t\tif len(expectedCodes) == 1 {\n1279\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes[0]\n1280\t\t} else {\n1281\t\t\texpected = expectedCodes\n1282\t\t}\n1283\t\n1284\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent error formatting\n1285\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1286\t\t\t\"status_code\",\n1287\t\t\texpected,\n1288\t\t\tactualCode,\n1289\t\t\tcontext,\n1290\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1291\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1292\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1293\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1294\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails - not applicable for status code validation\n1295\t\t\trangeInfo,\n1296\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1297\t\t)\n1298\t\n1299\t\t// Return the formatted error string\n1300\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1301\t}\n1302\t\n1303\t// getCategoryDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code category.\n1304\tfunc getCategoryDescription(category string) string {\n1305\t\tdescriptions := map[string]string{\n1306\t\t\t\"success\":       \"2xx - Successful responses\",\n1307\t\t\t\"redirection\":   \"3xx - status codes\",\n1311\t\t}\n1312\t\treturn descriptions[category]\n1313\t}\n1314\t\n1315\t// getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions returns relevant suggestions based on the specific status code received.\n1316\tfunc getStatusCodeSpecificSuggestions(code int) []string {\n1317\t\tswitch code {\n1318\t\tcase 400:\n1319\t\t\treturn []string{\n1320\t\t\t\t\"Check request body syntax and range of status codes for flexible validation.\n1411\t// This is useful when you want to accept any status code within a certain range.\n1412\ttype StatusCodeRange struct {\n1413\t\t// Min is the minimum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1414\t\tMin int\n1415\t\t// Max is the maximum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1416\t\tMax int\n1417\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of what this range represents\n1418\t\tDescription string\n1419\t}\n1420\t\n1421\t// Common status code ranges\n1422\tvar (\n1423\t\t// Range1xx covers informational responses (100-199)\n1424\t\tRange1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n1425\t\t// Range2xx covers successful responses (200-299)\n1426\t\tRange2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n1427\t\t// Range3xx covers redirection responses (300-399)\n1428\t\tRange3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n1429\t\t// Range4xx covers client error responses (400-499)\n1430\t\tRange4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n1431\t\t// Range5xx covers server error responses (500-599)\n1432\t\tRange5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n1433\t)\n1434\t\n1435\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange checks if a response's status code falls within a specified range.\n1436\t// This is useful for flexible validation where you want to accept any status code within a category.\n1437\t//\n1438\t// Parameters:\n1439\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1440\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1441\t//\n1442\t// Returns true if the response status code falls within the range, false otherwise.\n1443\t// Returns false if the response is nil or the range is invalid (Min > Max).\n1444\t//\n1445\t// Example usage:\n1446\t//\n1447\t//\t// Accept any success code (2xx)\n1448\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range2xx) {\n1449\t//\t    // Response is successful (200-299)\n1450\t//\t}\n1451\t//\n1452\t//\t// Accept any client error (4xx)\n1453\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, Range4xx) {\n1454\t//\t    // Response is a client error (400-499)\n1455\t//\t}\n1456\t//\n1457\t//\t// Custom range for specific validation\n1458\t//\tcustomRange := StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 204, Description: \"Standard success\"}\n1459\t//\tif ValidateStatusCodeRange(response, customRange) {\n1460\t//\t    // Response is in custom range\n1461\t//\t}\n1462\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRange(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) bool {\n1463\t\tif resp == nil {\n1464\t\t\treturn false\n1465\t\t}\n1466\t\n1467\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1468\t\t\treturn false\n1469\t\t}\n1470\t\n1471\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1472\t}\n1473\t\n1474\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails provides detailed status code range validation.\n1475\t// Like ValidateStatusCodeRange but with comprehensive result information including\n1476\t// range boundaries, distance from valid range, and contextual suggestions.\n1477\t//\n1478\t// Parameters:\n1479\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1480\t//   - range: The status code range to validate against\n1481\t//\n1482\t// Returns a context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx'), and suggestions for fixing the issue.\n1485\t//\n1486\t// Example usage:\n1487\t//\n1488\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(response, Range4xx)\n1489\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1490\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status code %d is not in range %s (%d-%d)\",\n1491\t//\t        result.ActualCode, result.Category, range.Min, range.Max)\n1492\t//\t    // MismatchDetails now includes:\n1493\t//\t    // - Expected range with description (e.g., \"400-499 Client Error\")\n1494\t//\t    // - Actual received status code\n1495\t//\t    // - Distance from valid range (e.g., \"99 codes below range\")\n1496\t//\t    // - Range pattern context (e.g., \"Validating 4xx pattern\")\n1497\t//\t    // - Specific suggestions based on the actual code\n1498\t//\t}\n1499\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, statusRange StatusCodeRange) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1500\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1501\t\t\tValid:           false,\n1502\t\t\tActualCode:      0,\n1503\t\t\tExpectedCodes:   []int{statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max},\n1504\t\t\tMatchedCode:     nil,\n1505\t\t\tMismatchDetails: \"\",\n1506\t\t\tIsClientError:   false,\n1507\t\t\tIsServerError:   false,\n1508\t\t\tCategory:        statusRange.Description,\n1509\t\t}\n1510\t\n1511\t\tif resp == nil {\n1512\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = \"response is nil\"\n1513\t\t\treturn result\n1514\t\t}\n1515\t\n1516\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1517\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid range: Min (%d) > Max (%d)\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1518\t\t\treturn result\n1519\t\t}\n1520\t\n1521\t\tresult.ActualCode = resp.StatusCode\n1522\t\n1523\t\t// Check range\n1524\t\tinRange := resp.StatusCode >= statusRange.Min && resp.StatusCode <= statusRange.Max\n1525\t\tresult.Valid = inRange\n1526\t\n1527\t\t// Set error flags\n1528\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500 {\n1529\t\t\tresult.IsClientError = true\n1530\t\t}\n1531\t\tif resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600 {\n1532\t\t\tresult.IsServerError = true\n1533\t\t}\n1534\t\n1535\t\tif !inRange {\n1536\t\t\t// Build comprehensive mismatch details with range boundaries, distance, and suggestions\n1537\t\t\tresult.MismatchDetails = buildRangeMismatchDetails(resp.StatusCode, statusRange)\n1538\t\t}\n1539\t\n1540\t\treturn result\n1541\t}\n1542\t\n1543\t// buildRangeMismatchDetails creates comprehensive mismatch information for range validation failures.\n1544\t// This function calculates the distance from the valid range, identifies the range pattern context,\n1545\t// and generates detailed error messages with suggestions using FormatValidationErrorWithDetails.\n1546\tfunc buildRangeMismatchDetails(actualCode int, statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1547\t\t// Calculate distance from valid range\n1548\t\tvar distanceInfo string\n1549\t\tvar distanceDetails []string\n1550\t\n1551\t\tif actualCode < statusRange.Min {\n1552\t\t\tdistance := statusRange.Min - actualCode\n1553\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1554\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code below range\", distance)\n1555\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1556\t\t\t} else {\n1557\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes below range\", distance)\n1558\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes below the minimum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Min))\n1559\t\t\t}\n1560\t\t} else {\n1561\t\t\tdistance := actualCode - statusRange.Max\n1562\t\t\tif distance == 1 {\n1563\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d code above range\", distance)\n1564\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d code above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1565\t\t\t} else {\n1566\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes above range\", distance)\n1567\t\t\t\tdistanceDetails = append(distanceDetails, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes above the maximum %d\", actualCode, distance, statusRange.Max))\n1568\t\t\t}\n1569\t\t}\n1570\t\n1571\t\t// Determine range pattern context (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\n1572\t\trangePattern := getRangePattern(statusRange)\n1573\t\n1574\t\t// Build expected range with description\n1575\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max)\n1576\t\tif statusRange.Description != \"\" {\n1577\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", expectedRange, statusRange.Description)\n1578\t\t}\n1579\t\n1580\t\t// Add pattern context to expected range\n1581\t\tif rangePattern != \"\" {\n1582\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s - %s pattern\", expectedRange, rangePattern)\n1583\t\t}\n1584\t\n1585\t\t// Build validation details\n1586\t\tvalidationDetails := []string{\n1587\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d %s\", statusRange.Min, statusRange.Max, statusRange.Description),\n1588\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actualCode),\n1589\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n1590\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", rangePattern),\n1591\t\t}\n1592\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails, distanceDetails...)\n1593\t\n1594\t\t// Use FormatValidationErrorWithDetails for consistent formatting\n1595\t\tvalidationErr := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n1596\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1597\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1598\t\t\tactualCode,\n1599\t\t\t\"\", // context - not applicable for range validation\n1600\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n1601\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n1602\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n1603\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n1604\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern: %s, Distance: %s\", rangePattern, distanceInfo),\n1605\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1606\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n1607\t\t)\n1608\t\n1609\t\treturn validationErr.Error()\n1610\t}\n1611\t\n1612\t// getRangePattern identifies the range pattern from a StatusCodeRange.\n1613\t// Returns patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '2xx', etc. based on the range boundaries.\n1614\tfunc getRangePattern(statusRange StatusCodeRange) string {\n1615\t\tmin := statusRange.Min\n1616\t\tmax := statusRange.Max\n1617\t\n1618\t\t// Check for standard century ranges\n1619\t\tif min/100 == max/100 && min%100 == 0 && max%100 == 99 {\n1620\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1621\t\t\tswitch century {\n1622\t\t\tcase 1:\n1623\t\t\t\treturn \"1xx (Informational)\"\n1624\t\t\tcase 2:\n1625\t\t\t\treturn \"2xx (Success)\"\n1626\t\t\tcase 3:\n1627\t\t\t\treturn \"3xx (Redirection)\"\n1628\t\t\tcase 4:\n1629\t\t\t\treturn \"4xx (Client Error)\"\n1630\t\t\tcase 5:\n1631\t\t\t\treturn \"5xx (Server Error)\"\n1632\t\t\t}\n1633\t\t}\n1634\t\n1635\t\t// For custom ranges, provide the pattern context\n1636\t\tif min/100 == max/100 {\n1637\t\t\tcentury := min / 100\n1638\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%dxx range (custom)\", century)\n1639\t\t}\n1640\t\n1641\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d range (custom)\", min/100, max/100)\n1642\t}\n1643\t\n1644\t// =============================================================================\n1645\t// ENHANCED ERROR MESSAGE VALIDATION\n1646\t// =============================================================================\n1647\t\n1648\t// EnhancedErrorMessagePattern defines a pattern for matching error messages in response bodies.\n1649\ttype EnhancedErrorMessagePattern struct {\n1650\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check in the response body\n1651\t\tFieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames\n1652\t\t// Pattern is a regular expression pattern that the error message must match\n1653\t\tPattern string\n1654\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether pattern matching should be case-insensitive\n1655\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n1656\t\t// MustContain specifies strings that must be present in the error message\n1657\t\tMustContain []string\n1658\t\t// MustNotContain specifies strings that must NOT be present in the error message\n1659\t\tMustNotContain []string\n1660\t\t// MinLength is the minimum length of the error message (0 for no minimum)\n1661\t\tMinLength int\n1662\t\t// MaxLength is the maximum length of the error message (0 for no maximum)\n1663\t\tMaxLength int\n1664\t}\n1665\t\n1666\t// ErrorMessageValidationResult provides detailed information about error message validation.\n1667\ttype ErrorMessageValidationResult struct {\n1668\t\t// Valid indicates whether the error message validation passed\n1669\t\tValid bool\n1670\t\t// Found indicates whether an error message field was found in the response\n1671\t\tFound bool\n1672\t\t// Message is the actual error message content that was found\n1673\t\tMessage string\n1674\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error message was found\n1675\t\tFieldName string\n1676\t\t// PatternMatched indicates whether the regex pattern matched\n1677\t\tPatternMatched bool\n1678\t\t// MustContainResults shows which required strings were found\n1679\t\tMustContainResults map[string]bool\n1680\t\t// MustNotContainResults shows which forbidden strings were found (should all be false)\n1681\t\tMustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n1682\t\t// LengthValidation indicates whether the message length was valid\n1683\t\tLengthValidation bool\n1684\t\t// Issues contains a list of validation issues found\n1685\t\tIssues []string\n1686\t}\n1687\t\n1688\t// ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails performs comprehensive error message content validation.\n1689\t// It Example usage:\n1698\t//\n1699\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1700\t//\tpattern := EnhancedErrorMessagePattern{\n1701\t//\t    FieldNames: DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames(),\n1702\t//\t    MustContain: []string{\"invalid\", \"token\"},\n1703\t//\t    MustNotContain: []string{\"password\", \"secret\"},\n1704\t//\t    MinLength: 10,\n1705\t//\t}\n1706\t//\tresult := ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(body, pattern)\n1707\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1708\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Error message validation failed: %v\", result.Issues)\n1709\t//\t}\n1710\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody interface{}, pattern EnhancedErrorMessagePattern) ErrorMessageValidationResult {\n1711\t\tresult := ErrorMessageValidationResult{\n1712\t\t\tValid:                 false,\n1713\t\t\tFound:                 false,\n1714\t\t\tMessage:               \"\",\n1715\t\t\tFieldName:             \"\",\n1716\t\t\tPatternMatched:        false,\n1717\t\t\tMustContainResults:    make(map[string]bool),\n1718\t\t\tMustNotContainResults: make(map[string]bool),\n1719\t\t\tLengthValidation:      true,\n1720\t\t\tIssues:                []string{},\n1721\t\t}\n1722\t\n1723\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1724\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is nil\")\n1725\t\t\treturn result\n1726\t\t}\n1727\t\n1728\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n1729\t\tfieldNames := pattern.FieldNames\n1730\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n1731\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n1732\t\t}\n1733\t\n1734\t\t// Try to convert to map\n1735\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n1736\t\tif !ok {\n1737\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is not a map\")\n1738\t\t\treturn result\n1739\t\t}\n1740\t\n1741\t\t// Search for error message in specified fields\n1742\t\tvar foundMessage string\n1743\t\tvar foundFieldName string\n1744\t\n1745\t\t// Check primary field\n1746\t\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1747\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n1748\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1749\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1750\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName\n1751\t\t\t\t}\n1752\t\t\t}\n1753\t\t}\n1754\t\n1755\t\t// Check secondary field if not found in primary\n1756\t\tif foundMessage == \"\" && fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n1757\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n1758\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1759\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessage = strValue\n1760\t\t\t\t\tfoundFieldName = fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName\n1761\t\t\t\t}\n1762\t\t\t}\n1763\t\t}\n1764\t\n1765\t\tresult.Found = foundMessage != \"\"\n1766\t\tresult.Message = foundMessage\n1767\t\tresult.FieldName = foundFieldName\n1768\t\n1769\t\tif !result.Found {\n1770\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"no valid error message field found\")\n1771\t\t\treturn result\n1772\t\t}\n1773\t\n1774\t\t// Validate length\n1775\t\tif pattern.MinLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) < pattern.MinLength {\n1776\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1777\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d is less than minimum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MinLength))\n1778\t\t}\n1779\t\n1780\t\tif pattern.MaxLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) > pattern.MaxLength {\n1781\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1782\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d exceeds maximum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MaxLength))\n1783\t\t}\n1784\t\n1785\t\t// Validate regex pattern\n1786\t\tif pattern.Pattern != \"\" {\n1787\t\t\tregex := regexp.MustCompile(pattern.Pattern)\n1788\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1789\t\t\t\tregex = regexp.MustCompile(\"(?i)\" + pattern.Pattern)\n1790\t\t\t}\n1791\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = regex.MatchString(foundMessage)\n1792\t\t\tif !result.PatternMatched {\n1793\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not match required pattern: %s\", pattern.Pattern))\n1794\t\t\t}\n1795\t\t} else {\n1796\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = true // No pattern specified, consider it matched\n1797\t\t}\n1798\t\n1799\t\t// Validate MustContain strings\n1800\t\tallMustContainFound := true\n1801\t\tfor _, mustContain := range pattern.MustContain {\n1802\t\t\tvar found bool\n1803\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1804\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1805\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustContain))\n1806\t\t\t} else {\n1807\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustContain)\n1808\t\t\t}\n1809\t\t\tresult.MustContainResults[mustContain] = found\n1810\t\t\tif !found {\n1811\t\t\t\tallMustContainFound = false\n1812\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not contain required string: %s\", mustContain))\n1813\t\t\t}\n1814\t\t}\n1815\t\n1816\t\t// Validate MustNotContain strings\n1817\t\thasForbiddenStrings := false\n1818\t\tfor _, mustNotContain := range pattern.MustNotContain {\n1819\t\t\tvar found bool\n1820\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1821\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1822\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustNotContain))\n1823\t\t\t} else {\n1824\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustNotContain)\n1825\t\t\t}\n1826\t\t\tresult.MustNotContainResults[mustNotContain] = found\n1827\t\t\tif found {\n1828\t\t\t\thasForbiddenStrings = true\n1829\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message contains forbidden string: %s\", mustNotContain))\n1830\t\t\t}\n1831\t\t}\n1832\t\n1833\t\t// Overall validity\n1834\t\tresult.Valid = result.Found && result.LengthValidation && result.PatternMatched &&\n1835\t\t\tallMustContainFound && !hasForbiddenStrings\n1836\t\n1837\t\treturn result\n1838\t}\n1839\t\n1840\t// =============================================================================\n1841\t// ERROR CODE DETECTION\n1842\t// =============================================================================\n1843\t\n1844\t// ErrorCodeMatch represents a found error code in a response.\n1845\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct {\n1846\t\t// FieldName is the name of the field where the error code was found\n1847\t\tFieldName string\n1848\t\t// CodeValue is the actual error code value (as a string for flexibility)\n1849\t\tCodeValue string\n1850\t\t// NumericCode is the error code parsed as an integer (if applicable)\n1851\t\tNumericCode *int\n1852\t\t// MatchedPattern is the pattern that matched this code\n1853\t\tMatchedPattern string\n1854\t\t// Position describes where in the response the code was found\n1855\t\tPosition string\n1856\t}\n1857\t\n1858\t// EnhancedErrorCodePattern defines a pattern for finding error codes in responses.\n1859\ttype EnhancedErrorCodePattern struct {\n1860\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check for error codes\n1861\t\tFieldNames []string\n1862\t\t// NumericOnly indicates whether to only match numeric error codes\n1863\t\tNumericOnly bool\n1864\t\t// ValuePatterns contains regex patterns for valid error code values\n1865\t\tValuePatterns []string\n1866\t}\n1867\t\n1868\t// FindErrorCodesInResponse searches for error codes in an HTTP response body.\n1869\t// This is useful for validating that specific error codes are present in error responses.\n1870\t//\n1871\t// Parameters:\n1872\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body (should be a map[string]interface{})\n1873\t//   - patterns: The patterns to use for finding error codes\n1874\t//\n1875\t// Returns a slice of ErrorCodeMatch representing all found error codes.\n1876\t//\n1877\t// Example usage:\n1878\t//\n1879\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1880\t//\tpatterns := []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n1881\t//\t    {\n1882\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\"},\n1883\t//\t        NumericOnly: true,\n1884\t//\t    },\n1885\t//\t    {\n1886\t//\t        FieldNames: []string{\"error\"},\n1887\t//\t        ValuePatterns: []string{\"invalid_token\", \"access_denied\"},\n1888\t//\t    },\n1889\t//\t}\n1890\t//\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(body, patterns)\n1891\t//\tfor _, match := range matches {\n1892\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Found error code: %s in field: %s\", match.CodeValue, match.FieldName)\n1893\t//\t}\n1894\tfunc FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody interface{}, patterns []EnhancedErrorCodePattern) []ErrorCodeMatch {\n1895\t\tvar matches []ErrorCodeMatch\n1896\t\n1897\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1898\t\t\treturn matches\n1899\t\t}\n1900\t\n1901\t\t// Try to convert to map\n1902\t\trespMap, ok := responseBody.(map[string]interface{})\n1903\t\tif !ok {\n1904\t\t\treturn matches\n1905\t\t}\n1906\t\n1907\t\tfor _, pattern := range patterns {\n1908\t\t\t// Default to common error code fields if none specified\n1909\t\t\tfieldsToCheck := pattern.FieldNames\n1910\t\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n1911\t\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n1912\t\t\t}\n1913\t\n1914\t\t\tfor _, fieldName := range fieldsToCheck {\n1915\t\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldName]; exists {\n1916\t\t\t\t\tvar codeValue string\n1917\t\t\t\t\tvar numericCode *int\n1918\t\t\t\t\tvar matchedPattern string\n1919\t\n1920\t\t\t\t\t// Handle different value types\n1921\t\t\t\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n1922\t\t\t\t\tcase string:\n1923\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = v\n1924\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"string\"\n1925\t\t\t\t\t\t// Try to parse as number\n1926\t\t\t\t\t\tif num, err := parseIntFromString(v); err == nil && pattern.NumericOnly {\n1927\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n1928\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1929\t\t\t\t\tcase int:\n1930\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", v)\n1931\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &v\n1932\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n1933\t\t\t\t\tcase float64:\n1934\t\t\t\t\t\t// JSON numbers are often parsed as float64\n1935\t\t\t\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n1936\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnum := int(v)\n1937\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", num)\n1938\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n1939\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n1940\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1941\t\t\t\t\tcase bool:\n1942\t\t\t\t\t\t// Some APIs use true/false as error codes\n1943\t\t\t\t\t\tif v {\n1944\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"true\"\n1945\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n1946\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n1947\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"false\"\n1948\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n1949\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1950\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n1951\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip unsupported types\n1952\t\t\t\t\t}\n1953\t\n1954\t\t\t\t\t// Validate against value patterns if specified\n1955\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched := true\n1956\t\t\t\t\tif len(pattern.ValuePatterns) > 0 {\n1957\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n1958\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, valuePattern := range pattern.ValuePatterns {\n1959\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched, _ := regexp.MatchString(valuePattern, codeValue)\n1960\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif matched {\n1961\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = true\n1962\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = valuePattern\n1963\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1964\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1965\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1966\t\t\t\t\t}\n1967\t\n1968\t\t\t\t\t// Check numeric-only constraint\n1969\t\t\t\t\tif pattern.NumericOnly && numericCode == nil {\n1970\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n1971\t\t\t\t\t}\n1972\t\n1973\t\t\t\t\tif patternsMatched && codeValue != \"\" {\n1974\t\t\t\t\t\tmatches = append(matches, ErrorCodeMatch{\n1975\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFieldName:      fieldName,\n1976\t\t\t\t\t\t\tCodeValue:      codeValue,\n1977\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNumericCode:    numericCode,\n1978\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchedPattern: matchedPattern,\n1979\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPosition:       fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s' in response body\", fieldName),\n1980\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n1981\t\t\t\t\t}\n1982\t\t\t\t}\n1983\t\t\t}\n1984\t\t}\n1985\t\n1986\t\treturn matches\n1987\t}\n1988\t\n1989\t// ValidateErrorCodeInResponse checks if a specific error code is present in a response.\n1990\t// This is a convenience wrapper around FindErrorCodesInResponse for checking a single code.\n1991\t//\n1992\t// Parameters:\n1993\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body\n1994\t//   - expectedCode: The error code value to look for (as string)\n1995\t//   - fieldName: The field name to check (empty string to check all common fields)\n1996\t//\n1997\t// Returns true if the expected error code is found, false otherwise.\n1998\t//\n1999\t// Example usage:\n2000\t//\n2001\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n2002\t//\thasInvalidToken := ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\", \"error\")\n2003\t//\tif hasInvalidToken {\n2004\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Response contains invalid_token error\")\n2005\t//\t}\n2006\tfunc ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(responseBody interface{}, expectedCode string, fieldName string) bool {\n2007\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n2008\t\t\treturn false\n2009\t\t}\n2010\t\n2011\t\tvar pattern EnhancedErrorCodePattern\n2012\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2013\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2014\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{fieldName},\n2015\t\t\t}\n2016\t\t} else {\n2017\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n2018\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"},\n2019\t\t\t}\n2020\t\t}\n2021\t\n2022\t\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody, []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{pattern})\n2023\t\tfor _, match := range matches {\n2024\t\t\tif match.CodeValue == expectedCode {\n2025\t\t\t\treturn true\n2026\t\t\t}\n2027\t\t}\n2028\t\n2029\t\treturn false\n2030\t}\n2031\t\n2032\t// parseIntFromString attempts to parse a string as an integer.\n2033\tfunc parseIntFromString(s string) (int, error) {\n2034\t\tvar result int\n2035\t\t_, err := fmt.Sscanf(s, \"%d\", &result)\n2036\t\treturn result, err\n2037\t}\n2038\t\n2039\t// =============================================================================\n2040\t// STATUS CODE RANGE VALIDATION (PATTERN-BASED)\n2041\t// =============================================================================\n2042\t\n2043\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt validates a status code against a pattern string.\n2044\t// It supports patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx' to check if a status code\n2045\t// falls within a specified range.\n2046\t//\n2047\t// Parameters:\n2048\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n2049\t//   - actual: The actual status code to validate\n2050\t//\n2051\t// Returns error if the pattern is invalid or if the actual code doesn't match the range.\n2052\t// Returns nil if the actual code falls within the specified range.\n2053\t//\n2054\t// Example usage:\n2055\t//\n2056\t//\t// Validate 404 is in 4xx range\n2057\t//\terr := ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 404)\n2058\t//\tif err != nil {\n2059\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %v\", err)\n2060\t//\t}\n2061\t//\n2062\t//\t// Validate 500 is in 5xx range\n2063\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"5xx\", 500)\n2064\t//\n2065\t//\t// This returns an error because 200 is not in 4xx range\n2066\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 200)\n2067\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(pattern string, actual int) error {\n2068\t\t// Validate pattern format\n2069\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n2070\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2071\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2072\t\t\t\t\"pattern in format 'Nxx' (3 chars)\",\n2073\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s' (%d chars)\", pattern, len(pattern)),\n2074\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern format: %s\", pattern),\n2075\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet\n2076\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName\n2077\t\t\t\t\"\", // location\n2078\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields\n2079\t\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails\n2080\t\t\t\t\"\", // rangeInfo\n2081\t\t\t\t[]string{\n2082\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern '%s' has invalid length\", pattern),\n2083\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern must be exactly 3 characters (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\",\n2084\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Got %d characters, expected 3\", len(pattern)),\n2085\t\t\t\t},\n2086\t\t\t)\n2087\t\t}\n2088\t\n2089\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n2090\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n2091\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n2092\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2093\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2094\t\t\t\t\"century digit 1-5\",\n2095\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%c' (ASCII %d)\", centuryChar, centuryChar),\n2096\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern century in '%s'\", pattern),\n2097\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet\n2098\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName\n2099\t\t\t\t\"\", // location\n2100\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields\n2101\t\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails\n2102\t\t\t\t\"\", // rangeInfo\n2103\t\t\t\t[]string{\n2104\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern '%s' has invalid century digit\", pattern),\n2105\t\t\t\t\t\"Valid century digits: 1 (1xx), 2 (2xx), 3 (3xx), 4 (4xx), 5 (5xx)\",\n2106\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Got '%c', expected digit between '1' and '5'\", centuryChar),\n2107\t\t\t\t},\n2108\t\t\t)\n2109\t\t}\n2110\t\n2111\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n2112\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n2113\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2114\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2115\t\t\t\t\"pattern ending with 'xx'\",\n2116\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", pattern[1:]),\n2117\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern suffix in '%s'\", pattern),\n2118\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet\n2119\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName\n2120\t\t\t\t\"\", // location\n2121\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields\n2122\t\t\t\t\"\", // patternDetails\n2123\t\t\t\t\"\", // rangeInfo\n2124\t\t\t\t[]string{\n2125\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern '%s' has invalid suffix\", pattern),\n2126\t\t\t\t\t\"Pattern must end with 'xx' (e.g., '4xx', '5xx')\",\n2127\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Got '%s', expected 'xx'\", pattern[1:]),\n2128\t\t\t\t},\n2129\t\t\t)\n2130\t\t}\n2131\t\n2132\t\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\n2133\t\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\n2134\t\tminRange := century * 100\n2135\t\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\n2136\t\n2137\t\t// Get description for the range\n2138\t\tdesc, _ := GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n2139\t\n2140\t\t// Check if actual code is within the range\n2141\t\tif actual < minRange || actual > maxRange {\n2142\t\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d-%d)\", pattern, minRange, maxRange)\n2143\t\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n2144\t\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s (%d-%d)\", pattern, desc, minRange, maxRange)\n2145\t\t\t}\n2146\t\n2147\t\t\t// Calculate distance from valid range\n2148\t\t\tvar distanceInfo string\n2149\t\t\tvar validationDetails []string\n2150\t\n2151\t\t\tif actual < minRange {\n2152\t\t\t\tdistance := minRange - actual\n2153\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes below range\", distance)\n2154\t\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails,\n2155\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d (%s)\", minRange, maxRange, desc),\n2156\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actual),\n2157\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n2158\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", pattern),\n2159\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes below the minimum %d\", actual, distance, minRange))\n2160\t\t\t} else {\n2161\t\t\t\tdistance := actual - maxRange\n2162\t\t\t\tdistanceInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d codes above range\", distance)\n2163\t\t\t\tvalidationDetails = append(validationDetails,\n2164\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Expected range: %d-%d (%s)\", minRange, maxRange, desc),\n2165\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Actual status code: %d\", actual),\n2166\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Distance from valid range: %s\", distanceInfo),\n2167\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern context: %s\", pattern),\n2168\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is %d codes above the maximum %d\", actual, distance, maxRange))\n2169\t\t\t}\n2170\t\n2171\t\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2172\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n2173\t\t\t\texpectedRange,\n2174\t\t\t\tactual,\n2175\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"status code validation failed for pattern '%s'\", pattern),\n2176\t\t\t\t\"\", // responseSnippet - not needed for status code validation\n2177\t\t\t\t\"\", // fieldName - not applicable for status code validation\n2178\t\t\t\t\"\", // location - not applicable for status code validation\n2179\t\t\t\tnil, // relatedFields - not applicable for status code validation\n2180\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Range pattern: %s, Distance: %s\", pattern, distanceInfo),\n2181\t\t\t\texpectedRange,\n2182\t\t\t\tvalidationDetails,\n2183\t\t\t)\n2184\t\t}\n2185\t\n2186\t\treturn nil\n2187\t}\n2188\t\n2189\t// ParseStatusCodeRange extracts the century digit and range from a pattern string.\n2190\t// This is a helper function for advanced use cases where you need the range bounds.\n2191\t//\n2192\t// Parameters:\n2193\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n2194\t//\n2195\t// Returns the min and max values of the range, or an error if the pattern is invalid.\n2196\t//\n2197\t// Example usage:\n2198\t//\n2199\t//\tmin, max, err := ParseStatusCodeRange(\"4xx\")\n2200\t//\tif err != nil {\n2201\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid pattern: %v\", err)\n2202\t//\t} else {\n2203\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Range: %d-%d\\n\", min, max)\n2204\t//\t}\n2205\tfunc ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern string) (min int, max int, err error) {\n2206\t\t// Validate pattern format\n2207\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n2208\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern format: %s (expected format: '4xx', '5xx', etc.)\", pattern)\n2209\t\t}\n2210\t\n2211\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n2212\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n2213\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n2214\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern century: %c (must be 1-5)\", centuryChar)\n2215\t\t}\n2216\t\n2217\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n2218\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n2219\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern suffix: %s (expected 'xx')\", pattern)\n2220\t\t}\n2221\t\n2222\t\t// Calculate Package validate provides helper functions for validating HTTP responses and other data.\npackage validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsValid checks if an HTTP response's status code matches one or more expected codes.\n// It supports both a single status code and a slice of valid status codes, making it useful for\n// validating API responses where multiple status codes might indicate success (e.g., 200 OK and 206 Partial Content).\n//\n// The expected parameter can be:\n//   - A single int (e.g., 200)\n//   - A slice of ints (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code matches any of the expected codes, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tsingleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 200)\n//\tmultipleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\tnoContentCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 204)\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tstatusCode := resp.StatusCode\n\n\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n\tcase int:\n\t\treturn statusCode == v\n\tcase []int:\n\t\tfor _, code := range v {\n\t\t\tif statusCode == code
